task_completion.description: Access the VitalDB dataset to locate Case 1.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the agent navigating to the VitalDB dataset (e.g., opening vitaldb.net in a web browser, or writing a script to access api.vitaldb.net).

task_completion.description: Download Case ID 1 from the dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the file for Case 1 being downloaded (e.g., a browser download notification for '0001.vital', or a terminal command executing the download).

task_completion.description: Open the downloaded case using Vital Recorder.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the Vital Recorder application open with the downloaded Case 1 file loaded into the interface.

task_completion.description: Ensure the download includes standard tracks like ECG and ART.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence within Vital Recorder showing the presence of standard tracks (e.g., 'ECG' and 'ART' visible in the track list panel or as active waveform displays).

task_completion.description: Save the file to the exact specified path and filename.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Visual evidence of the file saved exactly as 'C:\Users\Docker\Documents\VitalRecorder\vitaldb_case1.vital' (e.g., visible in a 'Save As' dialog, File Explorer, or terminal directory listing).

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually downloaded the file from the internet.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for genuine download activity (browser download manager, curl/wget commands, or python requests) rather than the agent just renaming or moving a pre-existing local file.

integrity.description: Verify the agent used the genuine Vital Recorder software.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for the actual Vital Recorder GUI being used to view the tracks, rather than the agent mocking the output in a text editor or terminal.

integrity.description: Verify the saved file is a legitimate dataset file and not a dummy file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look at file properties, download progress, or directory listings to ensure the file size is plausible for a medical dataset (around 21 MB), not a 0-byte or 1KB empty file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: VitalDB is officially hosted at https://vitaldb.net. Case 1 is a valid case (accessible via https://api.vitaldb.net/0001.vital) and its actual file size is approximately 21 MB (21,063,820 bytes) compressed. Note that Vital Recorder does not have a built-in 'VitalDB Browser' for downloading cases directly; it is expected and correct for agents to download the file via a web browser, API script, or VitalSync tool first, and then open it in Vital Recorder.
